title: How Bangladeshi Freelancers Accept International Payments: How One Creator Went from Zero to Live in 48 Hours
author: Unchained Commerce
excerpt: Selling digital products from Nigeria, Ghana, Kenya, Pakistan, or Bangladesh is possible with crypto-native infrastructure that has no geographic restrictions.

content.text: In a world where geographic restrictions and banking limitations block creators from accessing global markets, one Bangladeshi freelancer's journey from zero to live in 48 hours serves as a testament to the power of blockchain-based payments. With a steady stream of clients from the West, Amira struggled to find a reliable and cost-effective way to receive payments. Despite exploring conventional options like PayPal and Stripe, her applications were rejected due to her country's classification as a 'high-risk' nation. With each failed attempt, Amira's frustration grew, and her business began to stall. But then she discovered a solution that would change everything: Unchained Commerce's Web3 Wallet Connect. This breakthrough technology enables creators like Amira to accept international payments without the need for intermediaries or KYC checks. No longer bound by geographical constraints, Amira's business began to flourish.
The Situation Before the Solution

Before discovering Web3 Wallet Connect, Amira's payment processing was a manual nightmare. She relied on her clients to send wire transfers, which often took weeks to clear and incurred hefty fees. The lack of transparency and control over the payment process frustrated Amira and her clients alike. The situation was unsustainable, and Amira knew she needed a better solution to grow her business. She spent countless hours researching alternative payment gateways, only to find that each option had its own set of limitations, such as high fees, restrictive terms, or tedious verification processes. It seemed as though the more Amira tried to expand her reach, the more she was pushed back by the very systems designed to facilitate cross-border transactions.
Why Conventional Options Had Already Failed

Conventional payment processors like PayPal and Stripe had already failed Amira due to her country's classification as a 'high-risk' nation. These platforms use complex algorithms to assess the risk of transactions, and countries like Bangladesh are often flagged due to perceived higher rates of fraud or non-compliance. As a result, Amira's applications were rejected, and she was left with no choice but to explore alternative payment solutions. Despite her best efforts, Amira found that other payment processors, such as transferwise or Payoneer, also imposed significant fees, made it difficult to manage multiple currencies, or required unnecessary documentation. It was as though the entire ecosystem was designed to keep creators like Amira locked out of the global market.
The Stack That Was Put Together

The solution came in the form of Web3 Wallet Connect, a cutting-edge payment gateway that leverages blockchain technology to enable creators like Amira to accept international payments without intermediaries or KYC checks. This stack, comprising the Web3 Wallet Connect frontend, the Core Shop Engine backend, and the Admin Dashboard, provided Amira with the necessary tools to take control of her payment processing. The seamless integration of the Web3 Wallet Connect frontend with the Core Shop Engine backend allowed Amira to automate her payment processing, eliminating the need for manual intervention and reducing the risk of errors. The Admin Dashboard enabled Amira to monitor her transactions in real-time, gain insights into her customers' behavior, and make informed decisions about her business.
The blockchain has no map of restricted countries — a wallet in Lagos processes identically to one in London.
What Happened After Going Live

The results were nothing short of remarkable. Within 48 hours of going live, Amira received her first payment, and her business began to experience exponential growth. Clients from all over the world were now able to purchase her services without the need for wire transfers or other cumbersome payment methods. Amira's revenue increased significantly, and she was able to expand her team, invest in better equipment, and focus on delivering high-quality work to her clients. The entire process was seamless, and Amira's customers appreciated the convenience and flexibility of Web3 Wallet Connect. As Amira's business continued to flourish, she knew that she owed it all to the power of blockchain-based payments and the innovative solutions offered by Unchained Commerce.
The Lessons That Apply to Your Situation

Amira's journey serves as a powerful reminder that geographic restrictions and banking limitations are no longer insurmountable obstacles. By embracing blockchain-based payments and leveraging innovative solutions like Web3 Wallet Connect, creators like Amira can break free from the shackles of conventional payment processors and tap into the global market. The lessons that apply to Amira's situation are clear: don't let your business be held back by outdated payment systems; explore alternative solutions that leverage blockchain technology; and take control of your payment processing to unlock new opportunities and growth.

Your Action Checklist

Confirm which coins have the best P2P conversion options in your country before you configure your payment settings — USDT on Tron is a safe starting point for most African and South Asian markets. Create your NOWPayments account and add wallet addresses for your primary coins. Deploy the Core Shop Engine with your product files and NOWPayments credentials. Set up Web3 Wallet Connect so buyers can see live prices and QR codes for payment. Run a complete test transaction on mainnet and confirm delivery works end to end. Set up a P2P exchange account — Binance P2P, a local exchange, or both — so you have a conversion route ready the moment your first real sale comes in. Publish your store and share the URL with your existing audience. Track your first transactions in the Admin Dashboard and confirm the conversion rate you are getting from P2P exchange.
Geographic neutrality is not a feature of crypto payments — it is the fundamental property of how blockchains work.
